title: Certification Engineer - Aircraft Programs and Modifications
setting: An OEM, modifier, or specialist consultancy working with CASA, EASA, FAA, or defence approval pathways
companySize: Certification team of 5-20 supporting programs with 100+ engineering contributors
description: You live where regulation meets engineering reality. The job is part technical strategist, part evidence manager, and part translator between design teams and authorities. The work is document-heavy, but it is also deeply tied to real engineering choices.

schedule.activity: Review open compliance items and authority questions
schedule.note: The morning usually starts with evidence gaps, not finished work.

schedule.activity: Meet subsystem leads to align design changes with the certification basis
schedule.note: A small design change can create a surprisingly large compliance ripple.

schedule.activity: Update compliance matrices and means-of-compliance plans
schedule.note: This is where ownership and maturity become visible.

schedule.activity: Review a test plan or analysis report for certification suitability
schedule.note: The question is whether the evidence will stand up to scrutiny, not whether it simply looks technical.

schedule.activity: Prepare briefing material for an authority meeting or internal review board
schedule.note: Good certification engineers reduce surprises for everyone.

schedule.activity: Close the day by re-prioritising the riskiest open compliance threads
schedule.note: The hardest part is often sequencing evidence so the program can keep moving.

responsibilities: Interpret regulatory requirements for engineering teams
Coordinate evidence from analysis, test, and inspection
Identify certification risks early enough to influence design
challenges: Keeping traceability intact through constant design change
Translating technical nuance into approval-ready evidence
Balancing program pressure against regulatory discipline
rewards: Having a clear view of the whole program's technical maturity
Helping engineering work actually reach approval and service
Becoming trusted in a niche but high-value discipline
careerPath: Airworthiness analyst -> Certification engineer -> Senior certification engineer -> Certification manager -> Head of airworthiness
